mission.title: Install and Configure Clusternet on Kubernetes
mission.description: Clusternet is a CNCF Sandbox project that simplifies the management of multiple Kubernetes clusters, whether they are on public clouds, private clouds, or edge environments. By installing Clusternet, you can manage your clusters as easily as visiting a website, streamlining your multi-cluster operations.

steps.title: Add the Clusternet Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Clusternet Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add clusternet https://charts.clusternet.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Clusternet using Helm
steps.description:
Next, install Clusternet in a new namespace called 'clusternet'.
```bash
helm install clusternet clusternet/clusternet --namespace clusternet --create-namespace --version v0.18.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Clusternet pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n clusternet
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Clusternet deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment clusternet --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi -n clusternet
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the Clusternet API
steps.description:
To access the Clusternet API, set up port forwarding.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/clusternet 8080:80 -n clusternet
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Clusternet will show all pods in the 'clusternet' namespace running with a status of 'Running'. You can access the Clusternet API through port 8080 on your local machine.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add clusternet https://charts.clusternet.io
helm repo update
helm install clusternet clusternet/clusternet --namespace clusternet --create-namespace --version v0.18.1
kubectl set resources deployment clusternet --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi -n clusternet

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed to manage the installation.
